description: Versioned rename map. aa-upgrade walks the `renames` array, applies any entry whose applies_to range covers the project's current framework_version. The legacy top-level fields (`skills`, `agents`, `global_skills`, `global_agents`) remain for backward compatibility with the v6.0.0 namespace migration.

renames.description: v6.0.0 namespace migration — every skill/agent/script gets the aa- prefix.

v6.6.0: aa-task-flow-inspector renamed to aa-task-flow-progress-fixer. The new name reflects the skill's actual behaviour: reconcile workspace state against reality (PR merge state, branch existence, summary freshness), not just inspect.

v7.0.0: workspace skills moved from skills/ to workspace-skills/ in the framework source. The skills/manifest.json file and the run-from filter (v6.6.0-v6.10.0) are deleted; directory location now IS the role. From the target project's perspective the .claude/skills/ destination is unchanged, but the SOURCE directory aa-upgrade reads from differs by INSTALL_ROLE: code-repo installs read from skills/, workspace installs read from workspace-skills/. Also corrects aa-task-flow-remember mis-classification — it was workspace from v6.6.0 to v6.10.0 but is an in-session code-repo skill, so it stays in skills/ in v7.0.0. Code-repo installs that lost it under the v6.7.0 filter will receive it again on the next upgrade. File-level Add/Remove instructions for aa-upgrade live in the v7.0.0 CHANGELOG **Added:**/**Removed:** sections — no rename mapping needed here because the framework destination path under .claude/skills/ is unchanged for every skill.

notes.schema: The `renames` array is the canonical structure for v6.0.0+ migrations. Each entry has version (when the rename was introduced), applies_to (semver range the entry covers), and rename maps. Top-level skills/agents/global_skills/global_agents fields are kept for backward compatibility with aa-upgrade code that pre-dates the array schema.
notes.null_values: null means the item was removed from that install target (either deleted or moved to global)
notes.task-flow-record-improvement: Moved from project-level to global install (aa-record-improvement)
notes.ai-sanitizer: Renamed to aa-optimizer and remains global-only
notes.code-reviewer: Renamed to aa-code-reviewer (agent kept, only the generic framework agent name was ambiguous)
notes.init-tools: Was never a project-level skill — framework-operational only
notes.aa-task-flow-inspector: Renamed to aa-task-flow-progress-fixer in v6.6.0; the new name reflects the skill's reconciliation behaviour against actual git/GitHub state.
notes.v7.0.0-directory-split: Workspace skills (aa-task-flow-progress-fixer, aa-weekly-report) moved from skills/ to workspace-skills/ in the framework source. From the target's POV, the install destination is unchanged (.claude/skills/). aa-task-flow-remember stays in skills/ (was incorrectly classified workspace v6.6.0-v6.10.0). The skills/manifest.json file is gone — directory location replaces it.
notes.v7.0.0-remember-restore: aa-task-flow-remember was filtered OUT of code-repo installs from v6.7.0 to v6.10.0 due to its workspace classification. v7.0.0 corrects the classification (it's a target-project skill — invoked in-session when Claude forgets mid-task), so the next aa-upgrade against any code-repo target will RE-INSTALL the skill (treated as a new file because the target's .claude/skills/aa-task-flow-remember/ was deleted by the previous upgrade).
